page: Microsoft Azure F32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Faldsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This server features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities, with single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance in stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are rated in the top 25% tier. The server also demonstrates top-tier database operations performance in PassMark testing, alongside strong Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the F32alds_v7 is its low memory density relative to its high compute capacity, making it less suitable for memory-bound workloads. However, the combination of a high-frequency AMD EPYC CPU and substantial local storage provides high resource density for compute-heavy and storage-reliant tasks. This server is well-suited for database operations, web serving, data compression, and high-throughput computational workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure F32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, it presents a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive tasks but maximizes compute density. The server includes 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and database operations, alongside strong multi-core execution and web serving throughput. This configuration is highly efficient for compute-bound workloads, database operations, and web serving where high clock speeds and local storage are prioritized over large memory capacities.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32alds_v7 features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for high-performance compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and substantial local storage for intensive database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with a low 2.0 GB per core ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and database operations
